config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society’s interests matter a lot, but they should not automatically come before individual rights, because that can be used to justify abuse and silence dissent. In my view, a healthy society protects basic rights first, then asks people to contribute to the common good within fair limits. If the balance goes too far toward society, the individual becomes too easy to sacrifice.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan "demi kebaikan bersama" dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran hak. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi tanpa batas yang jelas pada kekuasaan, hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan privasi bisa dikorbankan terlalu cepat. Saya lebih percaya pada prinsip bahwa masyarakat yang kuat justru dibangun dengan melindungi individu terlebih dahulu, lalu membatasi hak itu hanya ketika ada alasan yang sangat kuat dan proporsional. Kalau tidak, mayoritas bisa saja menang terus dan minoritas yang menanggung akibatnya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the idea that society should automatically come first, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “for the common good” can become an excuse for pressure, corruption, or silence toward ordinary people. Social harmony matters in our culture, but harmony built by forcing individuals to give up basic rights is not real harmony. The state and society should protect the public interest, yes, but only within clear limits that defend freedom of speech, due process, and personal dignity. If individual rights are weak, then the so-called interests of society are usually just the interests of the powerful.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu tidak boleh ditempatkan di bawah kepentingan masyarakat secara otomatis. Argumen “demi kebaikan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, pembungkaman kritik, dan pengorbanan minoritas oleh mayoritas. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan privasi. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang benar-benar sehat justru melindungi individu terlebih dahulu, lalu membatasi hak hanya ketika ada alasan yang sangat kuat, jelas, dan proporsional.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement: in my view, society’s interests should never automatically outrank individual rights. From an Indonesian perspective, I have seen how appeals to harmony, order, or the common good can be used to pressure people into silence and protect the powerful instead of the public. Society does need shared rules and responsibilities, but those only make sense if basic rights like speech, due process, and dignity are protected first. If individual rights are treated as secondary, then “society” becomes just a convenient label for whoever has the most power.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu, karena di Amerika saya melihat betapa cepatnya dalih “kepentingan masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan pelanggaran hak individu. Kepentingan publik memang nyata, tetapi tanpa batas yang tegas pada negara dan mayoritas, hak dasar seperti kebebasan berbicara, proses hukum, dan privasi mudah dikorbankan atas nama ketertiban atau efisiensi. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menempatkan individu di bawah kolektif, melainkan yang melindungi hak individu terlebih dahulu lalu membatasi hak itu hanya jika ada alasan yang sangat kuat, jelas, dan proporsional. Kalau tidak, yang disebut “kepentingan masyarakat” sering kali cuma kepentingan pihak yang paling berkuasa.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
